page: Microsoft Azure E16ns_v6 is an Env6 family virtual machine configured with 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled and dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a large 260 MB L3 cache, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions.

Benchmark results place the E16ns_v6 in the average tier for most general compute and memory tasks. It achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1803.39 and a multi-core score of 13997.69. Memory bandwidth performance is measured at 1133.96 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 116.03 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Notably, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small models, reaching 350.76 tokens/sec, while maintaining average tier performance for larger model prompt processing.

This instance is network-optimized and premium storage capable, though it lacks any local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hypervisor layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, the E16ns_v6 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ns_v6 is an Env6 family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 8 physical cores with hyper-threading and a substantial 260 MB L3 cache. The processor supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 instructions. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it is network-optimized and premium storage capable. Benchmark data shows average-tier performance across general compute and memory bandwidth, with top-tier results in text generation for small language models. This memory-optimized profile is highly suited for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ns_v6 is a memory-optimized Env6 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and advanced AMX extensions, ideal for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ns_v6 is an Env6 virtual machine with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for memory-heavy workloads and databases.
tagline: A memory-optimized, network-ready virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector math
Network-optimized architecture with Premium Storage capability
No local storage or GPU resources included in the base configuration
Suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and web serving
